address: 114 Fifth Avenue, Tang Hall, York, YO31 0UW
lead_profile_text: Moving abroad end of June through at least winter. Specifically timing move to capture peak summer and autumn demand. 3-bed house in York, 15 min walk from city centre. No mortgage — all STL net is clear cash flow. Property has parking, garden, AC (rare for York summer), and a smart electric door lock. One bedroom converted to office but single bed retained. Recently renovated kitchen, hard floors.
emotional_profile: High-intent, time-aware, and pragmatic. No mortgage means all income is profit — clear financial framing. Timing urgency is the primary conversion lever: leaving end of June, needs to sign now to go live for July peak. Main risk is he overthinks it while abroad and goes cold. Smart lock battery dependency is a minor operational flag post-launch.
delay_trigger_phrases: Property live approximately 2 weeks after signing
delay_timeline: Leaving end of June. To sign agreement and send property photos immediately. Property live ~2 weeks after signing. Kickoff call to follow agreement.
green_light_trigger_phrases: Timing the move to capture peak summer and autumn demand
Property live approximately 2 weeks after signing
Snagging inspection will catch anything critical

top_objections.text: New listing disadvantage — York averages 4.8 stars and ~100 reviews; new listing starts at bottom of algorithm
top_objections.zac_response: Year 1 expected 10-20% below projections acknowledged; York case study (17 Park Crescent) showed strong results despite same ramp-up challenge

top_objections.text: Smart electric door lock practicality — battery needs monthly replacement, makes noise before dying
top_objections.zac_response: Operational flag noted for post-launch monitoring; key fob + app-controlled is generally more flexible for guests

top_objections.text: Personal belongings storage security with guests in the property
top_objections.zac_response: Attic for main items, lockable wardrobe in office room for sensitive belongings

top_questions.text: Is AC actually a differentiator in York?
top_questions.answer: Yes — Aaron noted it's rare for York in summer; it's a genuine competitive advantage that will show in listing performance during peak months.

top_questions.text: How does the new listing ramp-up work in York's competitive market?
top_questions.answer: York averages 4.8 stars / ~100 reviews. New listing starts at the bottom of the algorithm. Year 1 expected 10-20% below projections. York case study (17 Park Crescent) achieved £54,687 gross / £34,726 net in Jan-Dec 2025 — real results reassure on achievability.

top_questions.text: Can personal items be stored securely while the property is let?
top_questions.answer: Personal belongings go to attic; sensitive items in a lockable wardrobe in the office room. Guests do not have access to either.

what_moved_them: AC as a competitive differentiator — Aaron noted it's rare for York in summer
No-mortgage framing — all net income is clear cash flow (291% uplift vs LTL of £741/month)
York case study (17 Park Crescent) real results reassured on achievability despite new listing ramp-up
Timing urgency — leaving end of June, wants property earning during peak summer from day one
what_fell_flat: New listing disadvantage acknowledged — needed case study to offset concern

conversion_gaps.gap: Risk of going cold while abroad if agreement not signed before departure
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Create urgency: leaving end of June = needs to sign now for July go-live. If cold post-departure, time-limited offer could be the trigger.

conversion_gaps.gap: Smart lock battery dependency is a post-launch operational risk
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Flag to property management team pre-launch; establish monthly battery check-in protocol

zac_income_intro: Gross ~£66,900/year, net £34,785/year (£2,899/month avg). Monthly range: £1,983 (January) to £3,865 (August). Long-let equivalent: £741/month / £8,888/year net. STL net uplift +291%.
zac_reassurance_phrases: AC is genuinely rare in York in summer — that's a real differentiator that will show in your listing
17 Park Crescent achieved £54,687 gross / £34,726 net in 2025 — that's real money, not a projection
No mortgage means every pound above zero is profit — the maths is very clean for you
zac_analogies: 17 Park Crescent York (3-bed): £54,687 gross / £34,726 net Jan-Dec 2025 — used as reassurance case study
